flashcards.definition: A famous polling failure that surveyed 2.4 million people but predicted wrong due to selection bias and voluntary response.
The range within which the true population value likely falls; reflects sampling variability, typically ± a few percent.
A screening method to identify respondents who are actually expected to vote, improving poll accuracy.
Systematic error from people who refuse to participate, whose opinions may differ from respondents.
Combining results from multiple polls to produce a more stable and accurate estimate.
